surface.name: Netlify Start, Choose your path, with logged-in drag and drop as one fully reconstructed official route
surface.selection_basis: The current Netlify documentation home presents Create a new project and explicitly offers an AI agent, a Git repository, or drag and drop. Choose your path says there are many ways to publish and organizes them by whether code already exists. It does not select one universal route. The drag-and-drop path is reconstructed because the official page provides its complete ordered actions and a directly observable preview URL terminal, not because it is asserted to be canonical.

alternatives_considered.name: Deploy from a repository
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This official quickstart is for developers who already have a trusted repository and requires selecting and authorizing a Git provider. The docs home does not rank it above AI or drag and drop.

alternatives_considered.name: Create a new project with an AI agent
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This path requires a credit-based plan with AI features enabled and adds agent and prompt choices. The docs home presents it as a peer route.

alternatives_considered.name: Anonymous Netlify CLI deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a specialized CLI route for AI agents and automated workflows. It creates a temporary project that must be claimed within one hour.

entry_point.developer_intent: Publish an existing web project on Netlify and open its live preview URL.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has an existing web project folder containing HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or framework source.
The developer has a supported browser and reliable network connection.
For the logged-in drag-and-drop candidate, the developer has a Netlify account and can access the intended team. An anonymous branch exists but behaves differently for unbuilt source.
If the folder is dropped anonymously, it must already be built because Netlify publishes anonymous files as-is.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Your site should now be published on the web so you can share it with others.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Netlify has published the dropped project and exposed it at a unique preview URL ending in netlify.app.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The project dashboard shows the Preview URL and opening that URL displays the published web project.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Your site should now be published on the web so you can share it with others.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official Start from files sequence explicitly declares the site published on the web at step 4. Customizing the project name, subsequent manual updates, repository connection, domains, and other features follow that point.

prerequisites.requirement: For the managed drag-and-drop route, a Netlify account authenticated into the team that should own the project. The public docs link to signup but do not reconstruct its current atomic screens or verification order.
A current Chromium-based browser is recommended for drag-and-drop troubleshooting.
A local web project folder. A ZIP archive must be unzipped first.
If deploying anonymously or using manual deployment without a build, the folder must contain already built output. A logged-in drop can let Netlify detect a framework and run its build.
A current Free credit-based plan is sufficient for a first production deployment while credits remain. A production deployment consumes 15 credits.

primary_path.action: Open the Netlify documentation home and select Create a new project.
primary_path.output: The entry surface offers an AI agent, a Git repository, or drag and drop.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documentation does not designate one universal default route.

primary_path.action: Open Choose your path and select Start from files for a complete drag-and-drop candidate.
primary_path.output: The ordered Start from files instructions are available.
primary_path.success_signal: The page explains the account, Drop publisher, folder, and preview URL sequence.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A developer with a remote repository or no existing code may reasonably choose a peer route instead.

primary_path.action: Prepare the local project folder to deploy.
primary_path.input: A folder containing the web project's source or built output.
primary_path.output: A folder suitable for the chosen login state is ready.
primary_path.success_signal: The folder is unzipped and includes the project files; for anonymous publishing it already contains built files.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Deploys under 50 MB work best; individual files over 10 MB may cause a deploy to get stuck. Network interruption or insufficient free memory can stall the upload.

primary_path.action: Log in to the Netlify account and select the team that should manage the project.
primary_path.input: Netlify account credentials through the current login flow.
primary_path.output: The intended team dashboard is accessible.
primary_path.success_signal: The team-owned Projects page or Netlify Drop publisher can be opened.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A new developer must use the signup page. The public project-start docs do not enumerate signup fields, authentication alternatives, or verification states.

primary_path.action: Open the Netlify Drop publisher or scroll to the drag-and-drop section at the bottom of the team's Projects page.
primary_path.input: https://app.netlify.com/drop or the authenticated team dashboard.
primary_path.success_signal: The folder drop target is ready.

primary_path.action: Drag the project folder into the Deploy zone.
primary_path.output: Netlify receives the files and starts the first deploy.
primary_path.success_signal: The upload and deploy process begins.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A too-large project, files over 10 MB, unreliable network, outdated browser, or insufficient free memory can cause a stalled or failed deployment.

primary_path.action: For a logged-in source folder, detect the framework, run the build if needed, and publish the result; for prebuilt output, publish the supplied files.
primary_path.input: The uploaded project folder and current team credit allowance.
primary_path.output: A deployed project on Netlify's delivery network and a unique preview URL.
primary_path.success_signal: The project dashboard shows a Preview URL ending in netlify.app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A failed build produces no deploy. The docs provide troubleshooting based on the error message. Production deployment consumes credits and a Free plan has a hard credit limit.

primary_path.action: Open the Preview URL shown in the project dashboard.
primary_path.output: The deployed site or application loads from Netlify.
primary_path.success_signal: The intended project is visible at the Preview URL and the official guide says it is now published on the web.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If it is not publishing or not in working order, use the linked troubleshooting guidance and the displayed error. A successful publication does not guarantee application-specific correctness beyond the visible deployed files.

candidate_paths.name: Publish an existing folder through logged-in Netlify Drop
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This official path is fully ordered and ends in an explicitly declared published site at a unique Preview URL.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: It instantiates one peer choice from the docs home's Create a new project entry surface without claiming that Netlify ranks it over Git, AI, templates, frameworks, or CLI.

branches.condition: The developer already has a remote Git repository
branches.path: Sign up, choose Add new project and Import an existing project, select the Git provider, authorize repository access, specify detected or custom publish settings, and click Publish.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the file upload with provider authorization and continuous deployment configuration.

branches.condition: The developer wants Netlify to generate the project with an AI agent
branches.path: Use a credit-based plan with AI enabled, open Add new project, enter or choose a prompt, optionally select an agent and add files, then click Build now. The inspected Start section directs the developer to continue with Agent Runners but does not name a deployment-ready notification at this point.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds plan, AI enablement, prompt, and agent choices before the later Agent Runners continuation and deployment.

branches.condition: The developer does not want to log in before deployment
branches.path: Install and use Netlify CLI with `netlify deploy --allow-anonymous`; Netlify creates a temporary project with a live URL that must be claimed within one hour. Serverless or edge functions still require an account.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes the pre-deploy account gate but adds CLI installation and a one-hour ownership-claim deadline; anonymous drag and drop publishes files as-is.

branches.condition: The uploaded folder is prebuilt output
branches.path: Publish the supplied files without a build command.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids framework detection and build time, but requires correct local build output.

friction_gates.description: Netlify presents several peer starting routes and does not select a universal default.
The correct folder depends on whether the developer is logged in. Anonymous drops publish files as-is, while logged-in drops can detect and build a framework project.
The managed route requires login, but the linked signup flow is not atomically documented on the public Start pages.
The docs say deploys under 50 MB work best and that individual files over 10 MB can cause a stuck deploy.
Network reliability, browser version, and local free memory can block upload. The troubleshooting baseline suggests roughly four times the uncompressed project size in free memory.
Netlify may need to detect, build, and publish the project before the Preview URL is available. No typical duration is documented for this route.
A production deployment consumes 15 credits. The Free plan includes 300 monthly credits with a hard limit.

time_to_first_success.value: The official glossary describes Netlify Drop as making the site go live instantly and gives an example of obtaining a live URL in seconds; it does not state a numeric upper bound.

sources.title: Netlify Documentation
evidence_supported: Peer AI, Git and drag-and-drop routes

sources.title: Choose your path
sections_used: How to start your project's code?
FAQ for using the Drag and Drop publisher

sources.title: Deploy from AI code generation tool

sources.title: Credit-based pricing plans
sections_used: Credit consumption by feature and resource

sources.title: Netlify Glossary for Beginners
evidence_supported: Instant and seconds-level Netlify Drop time claim

uncertainties.question: Which of AI agent, Git repository, framework starter, template, drag and drop, or CLI is Netlify's single canonical first route?
uncertainties.impact: Different entrants face materially different plans, credentials, authorization, local tooling, and first-success terminals.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The documentation home and Choose your path explicitly present multiple routes and do not rank one as universal.

uncertainties.question: What is the current atomic Netlify account signup and verification sequence?
uncertainties.impact: The logged-in route can contain additional fields, authentication choices, email verification, terms, or team creation before the first deploy.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official quickstarts link to the application signup page but the public documentation inspected does not reconstruct its current steps.

uncertainties.question: Does the current logged-in Drop publisher always run a framework build for unbuilt source?
uncertainties.impact: The required local preparation and wait or failure states differ between source and output folders.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Choose your path says logged-in drops can detect and build, while Create deploys states that manual deployment does not run a build command.

excluded_after_success: Customizing the Preview URL and official project name.
Connecting a Git repository for continuous deployment.
Adding or buying a custom domain.
Deploy Previews, branch deploys, rollbacks, Functions, environment variables, redirects, and Forms.
Subsequent manual updates and production hardening.
